Comparison of cash flow statement presentation methods. Left side: Direct method with operating activities, investing activities, financing activities, and net movement in cash. Right side: Indirect method with net income, depreciation/amortization, working capital adjustments, and the same investing and financing sections.

Diagram showing four key elements in a cash flow statement: Net cash provided by operating activities; Changes in working capital; PPE Investment; Financing requirement/surplus. Each item is paired with explanatory text about business operating cash flow, working capital stress or control, PPE investment needs, and whether internal funds cover investments or require external finance.
